schemas.name: Virtual Auditor
schemas.description: AI-powered CA and IBBI Registered Valuer firm offering IBBI-compliant valuations (ESOP, FEMA, Income Tax, Companies Act, SEBI, IBC, 409A, complex instruments, AIF), FEMA compliance, GST and income tax appeals, forensic accounting, company registration, and secretarial services across India.

step.text: Assess nature and extent of contravention
step.name: Step 1

step.text: Prepare compounding application with supporting documents
step.name: Step 3

step.text: Receive compounding order and pay amount
step.name: Step 6

mainEntity.name: How much is the FEMA compounding fee?

acceptedAnswer.text: Up to 3 times the amount involved in the contravention. Actual fee depends on: nature of contravention, amount involved, period of delay, whether voluntary or detected, and mitigating factors. Our experience shows voluntary first-time contraventions typically attract fees well below the maximum.

mainEntity.name: Can compounding be done for all FEMA violations?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most contraventions under FEMA are compoundable. However, contraventions involving hawala transactions, trade-based money laundering, or national security concerns may not be compoundable and can face criminal prosecution.

3-6 months from application filing to compounding order. Straightforward cases (late filing with no pricing violation) may be resolved faster. Complex matters involving pricing violations or multiple contraventions take longer.

mainEntity.name: Should we wait for ED detection or file voluntarily?

acceptedAnswer.text: Always file voluntarily. Voluntary disclosure is treated far more favourably than ED-detected contraventions. RBI internal guidelines provide for lower compounding fees for voluntary disclosure, first-time offenders, and cases where the contravention has been regularised.

Delayed FC-GPR/FC-TRS filing, delayed FLA return, delayed ECB reporting, pricing violations (shares issued below FEMA floor), allotment to ineligible persons, non-compliance with sectoral conditions. Willful/fraud cases may not be compoundable.

RBI circular: based on amount involved, period of contravention, nature of contravention. Typical range: 1-3x the transaction amount for reporting delays. Lower for voluntary disclosure, higher for ED-detected. Interest for delay period also applicable.

mainEntity.name: How long does FEMA compounding take?

acceptedAnswer.text: 3-6 months from application to compounding order. Includes RBI review, possible hearing, and order issuance. During this period, the contravention is disclosed and being addressed — better than undisclosed non-compliance.

mainEntity.name: Can compounding be applied for retroactively?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Old contraventions (even several years old) can be voluntarily disclosed and compounded. There is no limitation period for FEMA compounding. Earlier disclosure generally results in lower penalties.

mainEntity.name: Should I compound or wait for ED detection?

acceptedAnswer.text: Always compound voluntarily. ED-detected violations carry higher penalties, potential prosecution, and reputational damage. Voluntary compounding shows good faith and typically results in 50-70% lower penalties.
